title: Discretionary and rule-making authority
description: Explain how the federal bureaucracy uses delegated discretionary authority for rule making and implementation
topic_path: How does the bureaucracy influence policy and society?

assessment_items.question_content:
In 2015, President Obama signed the Every Student Succeeds Act (ESSA) into law. The Department of Education was in charge of implementing the law. 

**Which of the following most likely occurred after Congress gave the Department of Education discretionary authority over the law?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Department of Education used its rulemaking authority to create regulations for states and school districts.
States and school districts sent their plans on how to implement the law to the Department of Education.
Academics and experts in education reviewed the Department of Education’s plans for implementation.
The president issues a signing statement to clarify the ambiguous parts of the education law.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is likely to occur immediately as a result of new legislation regarding transition programs for veterans of the Afghanistan War?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Department of Veterans Affairs will use its authority to decide how to implement the law effectively.
States will create several job training programs to help veterans find jobs as they transition out of the military.
The House Committee on Veterans’ Affairs will meet with recipients to determine the effectiveness of the law.
Military experts will make recommendations to Congress on how much money they should spend creating these programs.

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1971, President Nixon signed the Federal Election Campaign Act (FECA) into law. It required candidates for federal office to disclose who contributed to their campaigns and how much. Congress put the Federal Election Commission in charge of implementing the law. 

**Which of the following most likely occurred first after Congress gave the Federal Election Commission discretionary authority over the law?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Federal Election Commission enforced the law by creating a series of rules for candidates.
Political parties became solely responsible for raising funds for political candidates to meet the provisions of the law.
Congress determined whether the Federal Election Commission had the power to enforce the law.
The Senate ceded all power to make laws about campaign contributions to the Federal Election Commission.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Bureaucratic rulemaking is best defined as which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the power to make legally binding standards that all persons under the agency’s authority must follow
the authority to decide how to implement a law and decide Congress’s intention when it passed the law
the practice of hiring and promoting individuals based on their qualifications and job performance
the ability to settle debates between states and agencies on how to implement a law

assessment_items.question_content:
**Bureaucratic discretionary authority is best defined as which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the ability of executive agencies to decide the literal interpretation of the law and to take courses of action
the regulations issued by government agencies to implement public policy
the power of the President to create a law implemented by a bureaucratic agency without congressional approval
the supervision of departments and agencies conducted by congressional committees

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements explains why Congress gives the federal bureaucracy discretionary authority to implement public policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Bureaucrats are experts and can make the best choices on how to implement policy.
Bureaucrats are solely responsible for making regulations after Congress passes legislation.
Bureaucrats are responsible for appropriating budgets after Congress passes legislation.
Bureaucrats are the only group in the federal government who can interact with interest groups and the public.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios is an example of a bureaucratic agency using its discretionary authority?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Environmental Protection Agency issues a fine to a company for violations of the Clean Air Act.
The Department of Education meets with the American Federation of Teachers to discuss policy implementation.
The Federal Election Commission publishes a report listing every person who donated more than \$200 to a campaign.
The Department of Veterans Affairs sends officials to testify before a congressional committee.

assessment_items.question_content:
Congress passed the Clean Air Act, which gave the federal government the power to monitor and limit emissions of harmful chemicals into the atmosphere. However, in 2003, the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) announced that the Clean Air Act does not give it the authority to regulate carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases. 

**The EPA’s decision to not act is an example of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the power that individual bureaucrats and agencies have to make choices about how to implement existing laws
the power of bureaucrats to write the specific regulations that determine the implementation of public policy
the relationship between bureaucratic agencies, interest groups, and congressional subcommittees to promote a common cause
the collaboration between experts and stakeholders to create specific policy

assessment_items.question_content:
In 2010, Congress passed the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act, which gave the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) more power to regulate and enforce securities laws. In response, the Securities and Exchange Commission created the Office of the Whistleblower, which is responsible for gathering information from people who know of a company committing securities fraud. 

**The Securities and Exchange Commission’s ability to decide how much money it awards to whistleblowers is an example of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the power that individual bureaucrats and agencies have to make choices about how to implement existing laws
the power of bureaucrats to write the specific regulations that determine the implementation of public policy
the relationship between bureaucratic agencies, interest groups, and congressional subcommittees to create public policy
the collaboration between experts and non-experts to influence specific policy outcomes

assessment_items.question_content:
Every year, the Seafair Air Show performance takes place in Lake Washington in Seattle, Washington. To protect guests who are attending the festival, Congress passed a law designating the area a safety zone. In response, the Department of Homeland Security created a regulation requiring the Coast Guard to enforce the boundaries of the safety zone from 8 am to 4 pm. 

**The action taken by the Department of Homeland Security is an example of which of the following?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a guideline issued by government agencies, which provide specific details about how to implement policies
a written pronouncement issued by the President of the United States after signing a bill
a law created by the president and implemented by a bureaucratic agency without congressional approval
a permanent change to the Constitution of the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1975, Congress passed the Hazardous Materials Transportation Act, which gave the Department of Transportation the power to regulate the transport of potentially hazardous materials, like radioactive waste. In response, the Department of Transportation set regulations, such as requiring additional labels on shipment trucks that warn other vehicles that there is hazardous material in the truck. 

**The action taken by the Department of Transportation is an example of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a rule issued by an executive agency that lists specific regulations about how to implement a policy
the president’s platform to promote an agenda directly to the American public without consultation from Congress
the ability of executive agencies to decide on whether to take courses of action when implementing a policy
a hearing called by congressional committees to hear testimonies from heads of departments and agencies

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1963, Congress passed the Clean Air Act, which sets limits on air pollution on a national level. In response, the Environmental Protection Agency set standards to limit carbon dioxide emissions from new power plants in the United States. 

**The action taken by the Environmental Protection Agency is an example of which of the following?**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a directive from a bureaucratic agency establishing requirements on how actors in their jurisdiction can operate
an agreement between two legislators in which they agree to trade votes for each other's benefit
a coalition of experts inside and outside of the government who want to influence particular areas of public policy
a ruling on whether a law violates the principles set in the United States Constitution
